thesis: Live events plus embedded short-form video are the primary acquisition and retention loop.

items.title: Happening now
items.description: The pages below are the fastest path into changing information on the site right now.

items.title: Global earthquake watch
items.label: Monitoring
items.summary: USGS Earthquake Hazards Program is producing continuously updated public data, which makes this page a repeat-check surface.
items.meta: USGS Earthquake Hazards Program · May 11, 06:37 AM

items.title: Aurora watch
items.label: Monitoring
items.summary: NOAA Space Weather Prediction Center is producing continuously updated public data, which makes this page a repeat-check surface.
items.meta: NOAA Space Weather Prediction Center · May 11, 06:42 AM

items.title: NASA live programming
items.label: Watch
items.summary: NASA is already a direct watch destination, so the event page should keep routing visitors in and back out cleanly.

items.title: Next 24 hours
items.description: Upcoming launches and scheduled watch points that reward a return visit within a day.

items.title: CLPS Flight: Astrobotic Griffin-1
items.label: 51d 5h
items.summary: CLPS Flight: Astrobotic Griffin-1 is on NASA's published launch schedule and is a strong candidate for a source-backed launch event page.

items.title: Why people are checking this
items.description: The early click and revisit signals already point to live pages and embedded video, not generic category walls.

items.title: Live
items.label: 33 views
items.summary: Live pages work because the information can change between visits and there is a clear reason to come back.

items.title: Home
items.label: 24 views
items.summary: The home page should route visitors into live events and shorts instead of acting like a static section index.

items.title: Live Space
items.label: 28 views
items.summary: This page is already attracting more attention than the lower-signal utility surfaces.
